title: Fix ambiguity in adding localhost to podman save
body:
... As well as some naming decisions.
This change ensures podman save doesn't incorrectly prepend localhost when saving an image.

Followup PR for discussion in #1103 

Requesting comments because there needs to be discussion on how localhost will function in libpod. Is it going to be treated as a registry or a repository?
Further, if imageParts.hasRegistry is broken, what is a better way to check if pieces of an image name is a registry vs repository?

Signed-off-by: haircommander <pehunt@redhat.com>

comments.body: @mtrmac PTAL
bot, retest this please

LGTM
@mheon @umohnani8 @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
LGTM, though I'd like @mtrmac to give a look before we merge

> Requesting comments because there needs to be discussion on how localhost will function in libpod. Is it going to be treated as a registry or a repository?

I’m not quite sure what the question is.

Looking at things one way, a hostname specifies a registry (a server), and `localhost` is both traditionally a host name, and meant as an alternative to the `docker.io` registry specifier.

Looking another way, `docker.io/ns/localhost` is a valid repository specification, so I’m not sure what it would mean to “treat `localhost` as a repository” in libpod.

> Further, if imageParts.hasRegistry is broken, what is a better way to check if pieces of an image name is a registry vs repository?

My best guess is that we would eventually want to change `imageParts.hasRegistry` to work as expected — but I don’t _actually understand all of its users yet_, basically I am writing the tests like #1112 to document the uses and behaviors.

As for how to _actually perform_ the check:

- I think the logic we want is https://github.com/containers/image/blob/134f99bed228d6297dc01d152804f6f09f185418/docker/reference/normalize.go#L62-L67 == https://github.com/projectatomic/docker/blob/9cb56fd9a24a26e9d87725252e0023102c7ce5bf/reference/utils.go#L83 . (We _could_ decide on some different rules; but all of `projectatomic/docker`, `c/image`, `libpod` and `buildah` should behave consistently, and out of all these, I think `projectatomic/docker` (and `moby/moby`) have the strongest weight as precedents.)

- It should probably live in `c/image/docker/reference`, probably as a new `RecognizedDomainRegexp` or something like that. It hasn't been added yet because `imageParts` was the first really important user, I didn’t know `imageParts` had this problem until very recently. Separately, `c/image` will need something like that for parsing $mandatoryHostname[/namespaces…[/repo]] in configuration files, notably `sysregistriesv2`, so I think it’s time to add this. [_Changing_ `c/image/docker/reference.Parse` seems very risky, it would be a silent behavior change and the `c/image/docker/reference` package intentionally behaves exactly like `docker/docker/reference` for the shared APIs.]
@mtrmac addressing your comments, I added "/" to the prepend check and attempted to add comments to explain what was going on. PTAL
@haircommander CI is fixed in master, can you rebase?

> @mtrmac addressing your comments, I added "/" to the prepend check

One more place to update I think.

> and attempted to add comments to explain what was going on. PTAL

- `podman tag` is not really important here; `Runtime.NewForLocal` is looking up images by name, regardless of how that name was set up, and `podman pull` is one of the most likely ways. The overall
  > we need to check if localhost was added to the image name in NewFromLocal
 
  comment is correct in this aspect.

- So, saying what formats `podman tag` will create from its argument is irrelevant. (And, BTW, incorrect; `podman tag` only adds `localhost/` if there is not another domain specified, see `TestNormalizeTag` for examples.)

- The \~“default assumption” for looking up by name (without any specialized knowledge) is generally that the input and the name should match precisely; so, the comments ideally should explain \~all deviations from that in the innermost check (why `HasPrefix`, why `HasSuffix`, why the extra check for `:latest`.) — and given the above, by referring to `NewFromLocal` / `getLocalImage` behavior.

(I’ll defer to others as to whether the comment changes are blocking.)
